module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand FSD purpose
learningObjectives.description: Explain FSD as the financial output of transportation execution and charge calculation.

learningObjectives.title: Understand FSD lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Describe flow from POD and charge calculation through posting and payment.

learningObjectives.title: Understand finance integration
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how FSD integrates SAP TM with SAP S/4HANA Finance processes.

sections.content: The Freight Settlement Document (FSD) is the financial document generated by SAP Transportation Management after transportation execution and charge calculation. It contains all transportation charges payable to the carrier and serves as the integration point between SAP TM and SAP S/4HANA Finance for invoice verification and payment.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: The Freight Settlement Document is the final financial output of SAP TM and forms the basis for carrier payment.

After a shipment from Bengaluru to Mumbai is delivered successfully, SAP TM calculates freight charges using the applicable Freight Agreement and generates a Freight Settlement Document. The document is transferred to SAP S/4HANA Finance, where the carrier invoice is verified and payment is processed.

sections.title: Freight Order vs Freight Settlement Document
Best Practice
sections.content: Always validate charge calculation before generating the Freight Settlement Document. Incorrect transportation charges will directly affect carrier payments and financial postings.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An automotive manufacturer automated Freight Settlement Document creation for more than 15,000 shipments every month. Financial postings were completed automatically, reducing invoice processing time by over 70%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: Freight Settlement Document (FSD) is the document that integrates SAP TM with SAP S/4HANA Finance. This is one of the most frequently asked SAP TM certification questions.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse a Carrier Invoice with a Freight Settlement Document. The FSD is generated inside SAP TM, whereas the carrier invoice is an external financial document that can be matched against the FSD.

items: The Freight Settlement Document is generated after transportation execution.
FSD contains the final transportation charges.
FSD integrates SAP TM with SAP S/4HANA Finance.
Carrier payments are based on the Freight Settlement Document.
FSD is the final financial document in the SAP TM transportation lifecycle.
